steps.title: Identify the proportional-segments setup
steps.content: Three parallel lines $\overline{AF}$, $\overline{BE}$, and $\overline{CD}$ are cut by two transversals: one passing through A, B, C and one through F, E, D. By the Basic Proportionality Theorem, parallel lines divide transversals proportionally: $$\frac{AB}{BC} = \frac{FE}{ED}$$

steps.title: Test all four choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 16.8: Would require $\frac{9}{18.5} = \frac{8.5}{16.8}$, i.e., $0.486 = 0.506$ — not equal. ✗
  • Choice B — 17.5: $\frac{9}{18.5} = \frac{8.5}{17.5}$, i.e., $0.4865 \approx 0.4857$ — matches to nearest tenth (exact: 17.472… rounds to 17.5). ✓
  • Choice C — 18.4: Would require ratio $\frac{8.5}{18.4} = 0.462 \ne 0.486$. ✗
  • Choice D — 19.6: Would require ratio $\frac{8.5}{19.6} = 0.434 \ne 0.486$. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
